meta.description: Usage instructions, constraints, and dependency map for all SQL analytics functions
critical_rules: JSONB input key names differ by file: trend_analysis_functions uses {time, metric}; timeseries_analysis_functions and moving_averages_functions use {time, value}; mixing keys silently returns NULLs
calculate_cumulative exists in BOTH timeseries_analysis_functions AND trend_analysis_functions with different key contracts — always qualify which file you are deploying from
Custom PostgreSQL types (impact_parameter, likelihood_parameter) must be created before any function that consumes them
Functions backed by database tables (correlation/decomposition group, asset likelihood group) require specific table schemas to be present
adjust_pvalues_bonferroni accepts DECIMAL[] not JSONB — it is the only function in operations_functions with this signature
calculate_power_analysis accepts only scalar DECIMAL params — no JSONB input
calculate_moving_correlation and calculate_effect_sizes each require two separate JSONB arguments

installation_order.description: Functions must be installed in this order due to type and internal call dependencies

phases.label: Custom Types
phases.note: Types must exist before any function that accepts or returns them

phases.label: Batch and Comparison Functions
phases.note: These call multiple phase-3/4 functions internally

phases.label: Specialized Likelihood Functions
phases.note: Some read breach_method_metadata and risk_impact_metadata tables

phases.label: Asset-Level Aggregators
phases.note: Require phase-6 functions and database tables to exist

phases.label: Trend Analysis and Forecasting
phases.note: Install calculate_statistical_trend before forecast_linear and classify_trend

jsonb_input_contracts.description: JSONB input key names are NOT interchangeable — hardcoded in each file. Wrong keys produce silent NULLs.

timeseries_group.note: group field is optional; omit for ungrouped analysis

moving_averages_no_group.note: These functions do not support a group parameter

operations_with_condition.note: condition_column name is a runtime TEXT parameter — must match key in JSONB records

operations_prepost.note: entity_id_column and time_column default to 'entity_id' and 'time' but can be overridden

operations_sequential_cuped.note: Column names are runtime parameters — keys must match p_treatment_column, p_pre_metric_column, etc.

get_top_metrics_special.description: JSONB OBJECT (not array). Keys are metric names; values are arrays of {time, metric} records.

fields.inverse: BOOLEAN — true means higher value = lower impact

impact_parameter.usage_note: Always use build_impact_parameter() constructor — manual composite literals are field-order sensitive

likelihood_result.note: Return type — not typically referenced in user queries

database_table_dependencies.description: These functions read from specific database tables. Tables must exist with correct schema.

metrics_daily.note: Correlation/decomposition functions query this table directly — NOT pure JSONB-in functions

metric_definitions.note: Used to enumerate all metric names for correlation scanning

breach_method_metadata.note: Stores breach method definitions and weights

apply_impact_decay_function.note: Can be called for decay math testing but not a normal workflow entry point

apply_decay_function.note: Likelihood-side decay — result always capped at 1.0

calculate_category_impact.primary_purpose: Aggregates one category slice of impact_parameter[]
calculate_category_impact.note: Useful standalone but mainly internal to calculate_generic_impact

build_anomaly_explanation_payload.returns: JSONB (not a TABLE) — use SELECT func() not SELECT * FROM func()

compare_impact_methods.note: Diagnostic only — not a production scoring path

calculate_likelihood_trend.note: Heaviest dependency chain in the library — requires all likelihood sub-functions and both metadata tables

non_standard_signatures.description: Functions that deviate from the standard single-JSONB-input pattern

calculate_moving_correlation.signature: calculate_moving_correlation(p_data_x JSONB, p_data_y JSONB, p_window_size INTEGER DEFAULT 7)
calculate_moving_correlation.note: Two separate JSONB arrays — one per series. Both use {time, value} keys. Time points should align. No group_by support.

calculate_effect_sizes.note: Two separate JSONB arrays. Each uses only {value} key. Returns one row per effect size type.

adjust_pvalues_bonferroni.note: Native DECIMAL[] array — NOT JSONB. Syntax: ARRAY[0.031, 0.048]::DECIMAL[]. Output rows indexed by comparison_number (1-based) — no metric labels in output.

calculate_power_analysis.signature: calculate_power_analysis(p_effect_size DECIMAL, p_baseline_std DECIMAL, p_alpha DECIMAL DEFAULT 0.05, p_power DECIMAL DEFAULT 0.80)
calculate_power_analysis.note: Scalar DECIMAL inputs only. p_effect_size is the expected absolute difference in means. p_alpha and p_power are 0-1 fractions.

classify_impact_level.note: Scalar score input — chain after calculate_generic_impact or calculate_impact_from_json using CROSS JOIN LATERAL

calculate_likelihood_trend.signature: calculate_likelihood_trend(p_asset_id INTEGER, p_lookback_days INTEGER DEFAULT 90, p_params JSONB DEFAULT '{}')
calculate_likelihood_trend.note: p_asset_id is INTEGER. Reads historical data from database tables — not a JSONB-driven function.

get_top_metrics.signature: get_top_metrics(p_metrics_data JSONB, p_n INTEGER DEFAULT 5, p_ranking_criteria TEXT DEFAULT 'growth')
get_top_metrics.note: p_metrics_data is a JSONB OBJECT (curly braces) not array. Keys are metric names, values are [{time, metric}] arrays. Only function in library with this envelope.

build_anomaly_explanation_payload.note: Returns a single JSONB scalar — use SELECT func() not SELECT * FROM func()

calculate_cumulative.warning: SAME FUNCTION NAME in TWO source files with different input contracts

version_timeseries_analysis_functions.output_has: row_number, time_period, original_value, cumulative_value, cumulative_percent, cumulative_product, cumulative_max, cumulative_min

calculate_cumulative.recommendation: Deploy to separate schemas OR rename one function (e.g., calculate_cumulative_trend) before deploying both files to the same schema

trino_variant.description: Trino/Presto-compatible rewrite of the impact calculation engine
key_differences: No custom composite types — all inputs are JSONB
apply_impact_decay_function is an inline expression not a separate callable
No IMMUTABLE/LANGUAGE plpgsql — uses Trino SQL dialect
trino_variant.incompatibility: Do NOT mix Trino and PostgreSQL versions — they are not interoperable

find_correlated_metrics.input_type: Database table scan — no JSONB input
find_correlated_metrics.signature: find_correlated_metrics(p_primary_metric TEXT, p_anomaly_date DATE, p_lookback_days INTEGER DEFAULT 14, p_min_correlation DECIMAL DEFAULT 0.60, p_region TEXT DEFAULT NULL, p_product_tier TEXT DEFAULT NULL)
find_correlated_metrics.returns: TABLE(metric_pair, primary_metric, correlated_metric, correlation, abs_correlation, direction, data_points, window_start, window_end)
constraints: metrics_daily must be populated for the metric in the lookback window
metric_definitions must exist to enumerate candidate metrics
Raising p_min_correlation reduces output rows — no rows if nothing exceeds threshold
p_region and p_product_tier are optional dimension filters; NULL means all

calculate_lag_correlation.signature: calculate_lag_correlation(p_primary_metric TEXT, p_other_metric TEXT, p_anomaly_date DATE, p_lookback_days INTEGER DEFAULT 14, p_max_lag INTEGER DEFAULT 5, p_region TEXT DEFAULT NULL, p_product_tier TEXT DEFAULT NULL)
constraints: Both metrics must exist in metrics_daily for the lookback window
Negative lag_periods = other_metric leads primary (causal candidate)
Positive lag_periods = other_metric lags primary (consequent)

decompose_impact_by_dimension.signature: decompose_impact_by_dimension(p_metric_name TEXT, p_anomaly_date DATE, p_dimension TEXT, p_baseline_days INTEGER DEFAULT 7, p_comparison_days INTEGER DEFAULT 1)
decompose_impact_by_dimension.returns: TABLE(dimension_value, baseline_avg, anomaly_actual, absolute_delta, pct_delta, segment_weight, contribution_to_total, impact_rank)
constraints: p_dimension must be exactly: region | product_tier | region_tier
metrics_daily must have region/product_tier populated when using region_tier

build_anomaly_explanation_payload.signature: build_anomaly_explanation_payload(p_primary_metric TEXT, p_anomaly_date DATE, p_lookback_days INTEGER DEFAULT 14, p_region TEXT DEFAULT NULL, p_product_tier TEXT DEFAULT NULL) RETURNS JSONB
build_anomaly_explanation_payload.returns: Single JSONB value with keys: anomaly_stats, correlated_metrics, leading_indicators, impact_by_segment
constraints: Returns JSONB scalar not TABLE — use SELECT func() not SELECT * FROM func()
All three sub-functions must be installed
All table dependencies of sub-functions apply

apply_impact_decay_function.signature: apply_impact_decay_function(p_value DECIMAL, p_decay_function TEXT, p_decay_rate DECIMAL, p_time_delta DECIMAL, p_max_value DECIMAL, p_amplification_factor DECIMAL DEFAULT 1.0) RETURNS DECIMAL
constraints: Result capped at 10.0 for compound growth (not 1.0)
compound and square can exceed 1.0 — use with care in scoring contexts

calculate_category_impact.signature: calculate_category_impact(p_parameters impact_parameter[], p_category TEXT) RETURNS TABLE(category, category_impact, parameter_count, top_contributors JSONB)
constraints: impact_parameter type must exist
Only params where impact_category = p_category are aggregated — others silently ignored
p_category is case-sensitive

calculate_generic_impact.signature: calculate_generic_impact(p_parameters impact_parameter[], p_aggregation_method TEXT DEFAULT 'weighted_sum', p_scale_to DECIMAL DEFAULT 100.0, p_enable_cascade BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE, p_cascade_depth INTEGER DEFAULT 3)
calculate_generic_impact.returns: TABLE(overall_impact, direct_impact, indirect_impact, cascading_impact, aggregation_method, impact_by_category, parameter_scores, raw_scores, decayed_scores, weighted_scores, cascade_breakdown, calculation_metadata)
constraints: p_parameters must be built via build_impact_parameter() — raw composite literals are field-order sensitive
With p_enable_cascade=TRUE and cascading category params, scores can compound significantly

calculate_impact_from_json.signature: calculate_impact_from_json(p_config JSONB) RETURNS TABLE(overall_impact, direct_impact, indirect_impact, cascading_impact, aggregation_method, impact_by_category, parameter_scores, calculation_summary)

jsonb_schema.parameters: ARRAY of: param_name, param_value, param_weight, max_value, impact_category, amplification_factor(opt), decay_function(opt), decay_rate(opt), time_delta(opt), inverse(opt)

classify_impact_level.signature: classify_impact_level(p_impact_score DECIMAL, p_threshold_critical DECIMAL DEFAULT 90, p_threshold_high DECIMAL DEFAULT 70, p_threshold_medium DECIMAL DEFAULT 50, p_threshold_low DECIMAL DEFAULT 30)
classify_impact_level.usage_pattern: Chain after calculate_impact_from_json using CROSS JOIN LATERAL classify_impact_level(overall_impact)

calculate_cascading_impact.signature: calculate_cascading_impact(p_primary_impact DECIMAL, p_affected_systems_count INTEGER, p_dependency_depth INTEGER DEFAULT 3, p_cascade_rate DECIMAL DEFAULT 0.5)
calculate_cascading_impact.returns: TABLE(primary_impact, secondary_impact, tertiary_impact, total_cascaded_impact, affected_systems, blast_radius_score)

calculate_impact_batch.signature: calculate_impact_batch(p_asset_configs JSONB) RETURNS TABLE(asset_id, overall_impact, direct_impact, cascading_impact, impact_level, aggregation_method, parameter_scores, rank_overall, percentile)

jsonb_schema.each_element: asset_id(TEXT), aggregation_method(opt), enable_cascade(opt), parameters(array — same as calculate_impact_from_json)

constraints: asset_id must be unique within input — duplicates skew rank/percentile
Ranks are relative to the batch, not global

build_impact_parameter.signature: build_impact_parameter(p_name TEXT, p_value DECIMAL, p_weight DECIMAL DEFAULT 1.0, p_max_value DECIMAL DEFAULT 100, p_category TEXT DEFAULT 'direct', p_amplification DECIMAL DEFAULT 1.0, p_decay_function TEXT DEFAULT 'none', p_decay_rate DECIMAL DEFAULT 1.0, p_time_delta DECIMAL DEFAULT 0, p_inverse BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE) RETURNS impact_parameter
constraints: p_category typos silently mismatch in calculate_category_impact

compare_impact_methods.signature: compare_impact_methods(p_parameters impact_parameter[]) RETURNS TABLE(method_name, impact_score, score_difference, rank)

apply_decay_function.signature: apply_decay_function(p_value DECIMAL, p_decay_function TEXT, p_decay_rate DECIMAL, p_time_delta DECIMAL, p_max_value DECIMAL) RETURNS DECIMAL
constraints: Result always capped at 1.0 (unlike apply_impact_decay_function which caps at 10.0)
No compound or square options — use apply_impact_decay_function for those

calculate_generic_likelihood.signature: calculate_generic_likelihood(p_parameters likelihood_parameter[], p_aggregation_method TEXT DEFAULT 'weighted_sum', p_scale_to DECIMAL DEFAULT 100.0, p_normalize BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE)

jsonb_schema.parameters: Array with: param_name, param_value, param_weight, max_value, decay_function(opt), decay_rate(opt), time_delta(opt), inverse(opt)

jsonb_schema.type: Array of objects with asset_id and parameters array

compare_likelihood_methods.intended_use: Diagnostic — runs same params through all aggregation methods
compare_likelihood_methods.signature: compare_likelihood_methods(p_parameters likelihood_parameter[]) RETURNS TABLE(method_name, likelihood_score, score_difference, rank)

build_parameter.signature: build_parameter(p_name TEXT, p_value DECIMAL, p_weight DECIMAL DEFAULT 1.0, p_max_value DECIMAL DEFAULT 100, p_decay_function TEXT DEFAULT 'none', p_decay_rate DECIMAL DEFAULT 1.0, p_time_delta DECIMAL DEFAULT 0, p_inverse BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE, p_normalization_method TEXT DEFAULT 'minmax') RETURNS likelihood_parameter

calculate_breach_likelihood.signature: calculate_breach_likelihood(p_params JSONB) RETURNS TABLE(likelihood_score, contributing_factors, calculation_method, confidence_level)

calculate_vulnerability_likelihood.signature: calculate_vulnerability_likelihood(p_params JSONB) RETURNS TABLE(likelihood_score, critical_vuln_contribution, high_vuln_contribution, medium_vuln_contribution, low_vuln_contribution)

calculate_time_weighted_likelihood.signature: calculate_time_weighted_likelihood(p_params JSONB) RETURNS TABLE(likelihood_score, urgency_factor, dwell_time_penalty, time_to_due_bonus)

calculate_breach_method_likelihood.signature: calculate_breach_method_likelihood(p_params JSONB) RETURNS TABLE(breach_method, likelihood_score, method_weight, contributing_factors)

calculate_asset_likelihood.returns: TABLE(asset_id INTEGER, total_likelihood, vulnerability_likelihood, exposure_likelihood, behavioral_likelihood, time_weighted_likelihood, top_breach_methods JSONB, confidence_level, calculation_timestamp)
constraints: p_asset_id is INTEGER not TEXT
All four sub-functions must be installed
Both metadata tables must be populated for this asset

calculate_likelihood_trend.signature: calculate_likelihood_trend(p_asset_id INTEGER, p_lookback_days INTEGER DEFAULT 90, p_params JSONB DEFAULT '{}')
calculate_likelihood_trend.returns: TABLE(asset_id, current_likelihood, avg_likelihood_30d, avg_likelihood_60d, avg_likelihood_90d, trend_direction, trend_percentage, velocity, forecast_30d, calculation_details JSONB)
constraints: p_asset_id is INTEGER
Heaviest dependency chain — most error-prone function in the library
Historical likelihood data must span the lookback window
trend_direction: increasing | decreasing | stable

calculate_sma.signature: calculate_sma(p_data JSONB, p_window_size INTEGER DEFAULT 7, p_group_by TEXT DEFAULT NULL)

calculate_wma.note: Weights recent values more heavily than SMA

calculate_moving_quantiles.signature: calculate_moving_quantiles(p_data JSONB, p_window_size INTEGER DEFAULT 7, p_quantiles DECIMAL[] DEFAULT ARRAY[0.25, 0.50, 0.75])

calculate_moving_correlation.signature: calculate_moving_correlation(p_data_x JSONB, p_data_y JSONB, p_window_size INTEGER DEFAULT 7)
constraints: Two separate JSONB arrays required — one per series
Both use {time, value} keys
Time points should align between series
No group_by support

calculate_expanding_window.signature: calculate_expanding_window(p_data JSONB, p_operation TEXT DEFAULT 'mean', p_group_by TEXT DEFAULT NULL)

calculate_cumulative_operations.signature: calculate_cumulative_operations(p_data JSONB, p_operations TEXT[] DEFAULT ARRAY['sum','product','max','min'])

calculate_time_weighted_ma.note: No group_by support. Applies exponential decay weighting.

calculate_bollinger_bands.signature: calculate_bollinger_bands(p_data JSONB, p_window_size INTEGER DEFAULT 20, p_num_std_devs DECIMAL DEFAULT 2.0)
calculate_bollinger_bands.note: No group_by support. percent_b > 1.0 or < 0.0 indicates breakout.

calculate_lag.signature: calculate_lag(p_data JSONB, p_lag_periods INTEGER DEFAULT 1, p_group_by TEXT DEFAULT NULL)

calculate_lead.signature: calculate_lead(p_data JSONB, p_lead_periods INTEGER DEFAULT 1, p_group_by TEXT DEFAULT NULL)

analyze_variance.signature: analyze_variance(p_data JSONB, p_window_size INTEGER DEFAULT 7, p_window_type TEXT DEFAULT 'rolling', p_group_by TEXT DEFAULT NULL)

analyze_distribution.note: Does NOT use 'time' key — distribution is position-independent

calculate_difference.signature: calculate_difference(p_data JSONB, p_order INTEGER DEFAULT 1, p_group_by TEXT DEFAULT NULL)
constraints: p_order: 1 (first difference) or 2 (second difference)

calculate_rolling_window.signature: calculate_rolling_window(p_data JSONB, p_window_size INTEGER DEFAULT 7, p_operation TEXT DEFAULT 'mean', p_group_by TEXT DEFAULT NULL)

calculate_ema.signature: calculate_ema(p_data JSONB, p_alpha DECIMAL DEFAULT 0.3, p_group_by TEXT DEFAULT NULL)
constraints: p_alpha range 0.0-1.0; higher = more responsive; lower = smoother

test_stationarity.note: No group_by support. Returns single row with stationarity assessment and recommendations.

calculate_cumulative_from_timeseries.signature: calculate_cumulative(p_data JSONB, p_operation TEXT DEFAULT 'sum', p_group_by TEXT DEFAULT NULL)
calculate_cumulative_from_timeseries.duplicate_warning: See duplicate_function_names — same name in trend_analysis_functions.sql uses 'metric' key

calculate_percent_change.signature: calculate_percent_change(p_data JSONB, p_lag_periods INTEGER DEFAULT 1, p_method TEXT DEFAULT 'simple', p_group_by TEXT DEFAULT NULL)

aggregate_by_time.signature: aggregate_by_time(p_data JSONB, p_period TEXT DEFAULT 'day', p_aggregation TEXT DEFAULT 'sum', p_metric_column TEXT DEFAULT 'metric')

calculate_moving_average_trend.signature: calculate_moving_average(p_data JSONB, p_window_size INTEGER DEFAULT 7, p_type TEXT DEFAULT 'SMA')
calculate_moving_average_trend.note: Trend file version — uses 'metric' key. Different from moving_averages_functions.sql variants.

calculate_growth_rates.signature: calculate_growth_rates(p_data JSONB, p_period TEXT DEFAULT 'period_over_period', p_annualize BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE)

calculate_statistical_trend.note: Must be installed before forecast_linear and classify_trend

forecast_linear.signature: forecast_linear(p_data JSONB, p_forecast_periods INTEGER DEFAULT 7, p_confidence_level DECIMAL DEFAULT 0.95)
constraints: calculate_statistical_trend must be installed first

compare_periods.signature: compare_periods(p_data JSONB, p_period_type TEXT DEFAULT 'month', p_periods_back INTEGER DEFAULT 1)

detect_anomalies.signature: detect_anomalies(p_data JSONB, p_method TEXT DEFAULT 'zscore', p_threshold DECIMAL DEFAULT 2.0)

calculate_cumulative_from_trend.duplicate_warning: See duplicate_function_names — same name in timeseries_analysis_functions.sql uses 'value' key

get_top_metrics.signature: get_top_metrics(p_metrics_data JSONB, p_n INTEGER DEFAULT 5, p_ranking_criteria TEXT DEFAULT 'growth')
get_top_metrics.input_format: JSONB OBJECT not array — keys are metric names, values are [{time, metric}] arrays
constraints: Only function in library using JSONB object envelope — do not pass an array
Both calculate_statistical_trend and forecast_linear must be installed first

calculate_percent_change_comparison.signature: calculate_percent_change_comparison(p_data JSONB, p_condition_column TEXT, p_baseline_value TEXT, p_metric_columns TEXT[] DEFAULT ARRAY['value'])
calculate_percent_change_comparison.input_keys_note: JSONB records must contain p_condition_column key (group label) and 'metric' key (numeric)
constraints: p_condition_column is a runtime parameter — matching key must exist in each JSONB record
p_baseline_value is case-sensitive

calculate_absolute_change_comparison.input_keys_note: JSONB records need p_condition_column key and 'metric' key

calculate_prepost_comparison.signature: calculate_prepost_comparison(p_data JSONB, p_entity_id_column TEXT DEFAULT 'entity_id', p_time_column TEXT DEFAULT 'time', p_cutoff_time TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NULL)
constraints: JSONB records must contain keys matching p_entity_id_column and p_time_column
If p_cutoff_time is NULL, midpoint is auto-detected
Each entity must appear in both pre and post periods to appear in output

calculate_stratified_analysis.signature: calculate_stratified_analysis(p_data JSONB, p_treatment_column TEXT, p_treatment_value TEXT, p_control_value TEXT, p_strata_column TEXT)
calculate_stratified_analysis.input_keys_note: Records need p_treatment_column key, p_strata_column key, and 'value' key

calculate_bootstrap_ci.signature: calculate_bootstrap_ci(p_data JSONB, p_metric TEXT DEFAULT 'mean', p_confidence_level DECIMAL DEFAULT 95.0, p_bootstrap_samples INTEGER DEFAULT 1000)
constraints: p_confidence_level is 0-100 (not 0-1) — use 95.0 not 0.95
Minimum 1000 bootstrap samples recommended for accuracy

calculate_power_analysis.signature: calculate_power_analysis(p_effect_size DECIMAL, p_baseline_std DECIMAL, p_alpha DECIMAL DEFAULT 0.05, p_power DECIMAL DEFAULT 0.80)
calculate_power_analysis.note: Scalar inputs only — no JSONB. p_alpha and p_power are 0-1 fractions not percentages.

calculate_effect_sizes.returns: One row per type: cohens_d, hedges_g, glass_delta with interpretation
constraints: Two separate JSONB arrays required
DECIMAL[] array not JSONB — syntax: ARRAY[0.031, 0.048]::DECIMAL[]
Output rows are indexed by comparison_number (1-based) only — maintain metric label mapping externally

calculate_sequential_analysis.signature: calculate_sequential_analysis(p_data JSONB, p_treatment_column TEXT, p_treatment_value TEXT, p_control_value TEXT, p_alpha DECIMAL DEFAULT 0.05)
calculate_sequential_analysis.input_keys_note: JSONB records need p_treatment_column key and 'value' key
calculate_sequential_analysis.purpose: A/B test early stopping — implements O'Brien-Fleming boundary

calculate_cuped_adjustment.signature: calculate_cuped_adjustment(p_data JSONB, p_treatment_column TEXT, p_pre_metric_column TEXT DEFAULT 'pre_value', p_post_metric_column TEXT DEFAULT 'post_value')
calculate_cuped_adjustment.input_keys_note: Records need p_treatment_column, p_pre_metric_column, and p_post_metric_column keys
calculate_cuped_adjustment.purpose: Variance reduction using pre-experiment covariate — increases experiment sensitivity
